name: Leap Quest

notes.en: Touch-first: hold LEFT / RIGHT (bottom-left) to run and tap JUMP (bottom-right) to hop — hold JUMP longer for a higher leap (a quick tap is a short hop). Slide down a wall and tap JUMP to wall-jump off it — the only way up the tall shaft in level 4. Land on an enemy's head to stomp it (you bounce); touching one from the side costs a life. Desktop bonus: Arrow keys or A / D to move, Space / W / Up to jump.
notes.ru: Приоритет — сенсор: удерживай ВЛЕВО / ВПРАВО (внизу слева) для бега и нажимай ПРЫЖОК (внизу справа) — держи ПРЫЖОК дольше для более высокого прыжка (короткое нажатие — низкий прыжок). Скользи по стене и нажми ПРЫЖОК, чтобы оттолкнуться — единственный путь наверх по высокой шахте на 4-м уровне. Приземлись врагу на голову, чтобы его сбить (ты отскочишь); касание сбоку стоит жизни. Бонус для ПК: стрелки или A / D — движение, Пробел / W / Вверх — прыжок.

notes: Original side-scrolling platformer in the classic 'run, jump on enemies to defeat them, collect coins, reach the flag' 2D tradition (no copied content — original neon-vector art, levels and character). Momentum-based movement: gravity-driven variable-height jumping (a tap is a short hop, holding JUMP through the rise is a full ~3-tile leap — the release cuts remaining upward velocity), run with real acceleration/deceleration, and one extra movement ability — the WALL-JUMP: press into a wall while falling to wall-slide (fall speed clamped), then JUMP to kick up and away, alternating off two close walls to climb a shaft. Level 4 'The Ascent' is a tall two-walled shaft where wall-jumping is the only route to the exit and flag (not optional), with a shorter nook in level 5 too. client/player.js owns the body physics and axis-separated AABB tile collision plus one-way land-on-top moving-platform collision that carries a rider; client/enemies.js has two behaviours (CRAWLER back-and-forth patroller that reverses at edges/walls; STALKER that charges ~3x speed when the player enters a detection box at similar height but still respects edges), both stomped from above and harmful on side/below contact. client/levels.js authors 5 hand-tuned levels from numeric rects (not fragile ASCII), with a reachability lint warning on any un-bridged pit wider than the jump budget; hazards are spikes (a hit), bottomless pits (a life), and moving platforms. A star-shell power-up grants one extra hit (removed on the next hit). 3 lives, coins/stomps/level-clear score, results screen on game over or full clear, local high score via OGHProfile (games/pop-the-bugs convention). Smoothed follow-camera clamped to level bounds, translate-the-world shape as games/hill-rider / games/neon-drift (follows vertically for the shaft). No bundled image/audio assets: Canvas 2-D neon-vector line-art with glow; sound reuses games/_shared/js/ogh-sfx.js entirely unchanged (hop=jump, boing=wall-jump, thwack=stomp, pickup=coin, chain=power-up, screech=lose shell, die=lose life, win=level clear/victory). RTL (Arabic) flips text/menu chrome only; the level, camera, run direction and LEFT/RIGHT/JUMP button layout never mirror (stage dir=ltr, ctx.direction forced ltr).
